root_text.an4.10:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco kāmānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.10:1.6: Tassa kāmānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ appajānato yo kāmesu kāmarāgo kāmanandī kāmasneho kāmamucchā kāmapipāsā kāmapariḷāho kāmajjhosānaṁ kāmataṇhā sānuseti.
root_text.an4.10:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco bhavānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.10:2.3: Tassa bhavānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ appajānato yo bhavesu bhavarāgo bhavanandī bhavasneho bhavamucchā bhavapipāsā bhavapariḷāho bhavajjhosānaṁ bhavataṇhā sānuseti.
root_text.an4.10:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco diṭṭhīnaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.10:3.3: Tassa diṭṭhīnaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ appajānato yo diṭṭhīsu diṭṭhirāgo diṭṭhinandī diṭṭhisneho diṭṭhimucchā diṭṭhipipāsā diṭṭhipariḷāho diṭṭhijjhosānaṁ diṭṭhitaṇhā sānuseti.
root_text.an4.10:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.10:4.3: Tassa channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ appajānato yā chasu phassāyatanesu avijjā aññāṇaṁ sānuseti.
root_text.an4.10:4.6: saṁyutto pāpakehi akusalehi dhammehi saṅkilesikehi ponobhavikehi sadarehi dukkhavipākehi āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇikehi. Tasmā ayogakkhemīti vuccati.
root_text.an4.10:5.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco kāmānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.10:5.6: Tassa kāmānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānato yo kāmesu kāmarāgo kāmanandī kāmasneho kāmamucchā kāmapipāsā kāmapariḷāho kāmajjhosānaṁ kāmataṇhā sā nānuseti.
root_text.an4.10:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco bhavānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.10:6.3: Tassa bhavānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānato yo bhavesu bhavarāgo bhavanandī bhavasneho bhavamucchā bhavapipāsā bhavapariḷāho bhavajjhosānaṁ bhavataṇhā sā nānuseti.
root_text.an4.10:7.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco diṭṭhīnaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.10:7.3: Tassa diṭṭhīnaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānato yo diṭṭhīsu diṭṭhirāgo diṭṭhinandī diṭṭhisneho diṭṭhimucchā diṭṭhipipāsā diṭṭhipariḷāho diṭṭhijjhosānaṁ diṭṭhitaṇhā sā nānuseti.
root_text.an4.10:8.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.10:8.3: Tassa channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānato yā chasu phassāyatanesu avijjā aññāṇaṁ sā nānuseti.
root_text.an4.10:8.6: visaṁyutto pāpakehi akusalehi dhammehi saṅkilesikehi ponobhavikehi sadarehi dukkhavipākehi āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇikehi. Tasmā yogakkhemīti vuccati.

translation_text.an4.10:1.3: The yokes of sensual pleasures, future lives, views, and ignorance.
translation_text.an4.10:1.4: And what is the yoke of sensual pleasures?
translation_text.an4.10:1.5: It’s when you don’t truly understand sensual pleasures’ origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.
translation_text.an4.10:1.6: So greed, relishing, affection, infatuation, thirst, passion, attachment, and craving for sensual pleasures linger on inside.
translation_text.an4.10:1.7: This is called the yoke of sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an4.10:1.8: Such is the yoke of sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an4.10:2.1: And what is the yoke of future lives?
translation_text.an4.10:2.2: It’s when you don’t truly understand future lives’ origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.
translation_text.an4.10:2.3: So greed, relishing, affection, infatuation, thirst, passion, attachment, and craving for continued existence linger on inside.
translation_text.an4.10:2.4: This is called the yoke of future lives.
translation_text.an4.10:2.5: Such are the yokes of sensual pleasures and future lives.
translation_text.an4.10:3.1: And what is the yoke of views?
translation_text.an4.10:3.2: It’s when you don’t truly understand views’ origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.
translation_text.an4.10:3.3: So greed, relishing, affection, infatuation, thirst, passion, attachment, and craving for views linger on inside.
translation_text.an4.10:3.4: This is called the yoke of views.
translation_text.an4.10:3.5: Such are the yokes of sensual pleasures, future lives, and views.
translation_text.an4.10:4.1: And what is the yoke of ignorance?
translation_text.an4.10:4.2: It’s when you don’t truly understand the six fields of contact’s origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape,
translation_text.an4.10:4.3: so ignorance and unknowing of the six fields of contact linger on inside.
translation_text.an4.10:4.4: This is called the yoke of ignorance.
translation_text.an4.10:4.5: Such are the yokes of sensual pleasures, future lives, views, and ignorance.
translation_text.an4.10:4.6: These are bad, unskillful qualities that are corrupting, leading to future lives, hurtful, resulting in suffering and future rebirth, old age, and death. That’s why someone yoked to them is called: ‘one who has not found sanctuary from the yoke’.
translation_text.an4.10:5.1: There are these four kinds of unyoking.
translation_text.an4.10:5.3: Unyoking from sensual pleasures, future lives, views, and ignorance.
translation_text.an4.10:5.4: And what is unyoking from sensual pleasures?
translation_text.an4.10:5.5: It’s when you truly understand sensual pleasures’ origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.
translation_text.an4.10:5.6: So greed, relishing, affection, infatuation, thirst, passion, attachment, and craving for sensual pleasures don’t linger on inside.
translation_text.an4.10:5.7: This is called unyoking from sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an4.10:5.8: Such is unyoking from sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an4.10:6.1: And what is unyoking from future lives?
translation_text.an4.10:6.2: It’s when you truly understand future lives’ origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.
translation_text.an4.10:6.3: So greed, relishing, affection, infatuation, thirst, passion, attachment, and craving for continued existence don’t linger on inside.
translation_text.an4.10:6.4: This is called unyoking from future lives.
translation_text.an4.10:6.5: Such is unyoking from sensual pleasures and future lives.
translation_text.an4.10:7.1: And what is unyoking from views?
translation_text.an4.10:7.2: It’s when you truly understand views’ origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.
translation_text.an4.10:7.3: So greed, relishing, affection, infatuation, thirst, passion, attachment, and craving for views don’t linger on inside.
translation_text.an4.10:7.4: This is called unyoking from views.
translation_text.an4.10:7.5: Such is unyoking from sensual pleasures, future lives, and views.
translation_text.an4.10:8.1: And what is unyoking from ignorance?
translation_text.an4.10:8.2: It’s when you truly understand the six fields of contact’s origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape,
translation_text.an4.10:8.3: so ignorance and unknowing of the six fields of contact don’t linger on inside.
translation_text.an4.10:8.4: This is called unyoking from ignorance.
translation_text.an4.10:8.5: Such is unyoking from sensual pleasures, future lives, views, and ignorance.
translation_text.an4.10:8.6: These are bad, unskillful qualities that are corrupting, leading to future lives, hurtful, resulting in suffering and future rebirth, old age, and death. That’s why someone unyoked from them is called: ‘one who has found sanctuary from the yoke’.
translation_text.an4.10:8.7: These are the four kinds of unyoking.
translation_text.an4.10:9.2: and the desire to be reborn in a future life;
translation_text.an4.10:10.3: But those who fully understand sensual pleasures,
translation_text.an4.10:10.4: and the yoke to all future lives;
translation_text.an4.10:11.1: with the yoke of views eradicated,
translation_text.an4.10:11.4: truly those sages have slipped their yoke.”

variant_text.an4.10:1.6: appajānato → nappajānato (sya-all, km, mr) | kāmanandī → kāmanandi (bj, sya-all, km) | kāmasneho → kāmasineho (bj, sya-all)
